yeah.

and landed here at growthx recently.

think we still have to add you on this.

Just quickly, covering the LLM side of things, we didn't see too many changes here.
Generally, we're still getting the majority of our referrals from perplexity.
We have that initial bump from Mistral in the earlier weeks.
Since then, we've seen...
So I'm not quite sure what's happening there, but it was a significant bump early on.
And again, the number of NGATE sessions over here compared to NGATE sessions again is just, you know, how long it's going to spend on page or clicks on the cost of the website.
The numbers are quite promising.
So we seem to be definitely creating, it's kind of a marker of the equality of content.
As long as the number of NGATE sessions are pretty high, it's quite happy to see it at this point.
Any questions I can answer about any of this?

Let me just move it up.
I just wanted to make sure I'm understanding this correctly.
It was in this particular thing, which was SQL help.
So you mentioned, yeah, are we helping people get better in SQL or enable any of tools to use data that just happens to be a Databricks SQL?
So I just want to clarify, like, what you mean by that.
Let me catch up.
The point I'm making there is to our audience, when we're thinking about the shift we're trying to make.
From data engineering to enabling wider cloud.
right side.
The
That's the business.
How much do we want to go into, like, SQL is a thing.
You need to do joins.
When was the last time you used a left join?
Versus, okay, SQL's out there.
Here's how you can use SQL.
Databricks SQL is this thing that you can use.
There's a specific context about Databricks SQL, how it fits in the data lake house.
Yeah.
It's kind of this forward staging, place where you can get into a familiar language, place where analysts can come in and use.

It's, yeah, I think our focus should be on using SQL, using Databricks SQL in specifics to allow people to get faster access to data, or it's sort of well-governed and has all this stuff.
Versus, hey, when you do a join, think about it this way, or...
One was the last, because there's the whole, I mean, you know, whatever it is, I don't know how old SQL is, 45 years?
It's old.
So we don't, I don't think we need to climb that mountain.
I think we need to talk about analysts and why they use SQL.
That's a great one.
Hey, why is SQL still the common, most popular language?
Well, it is because this next generation is taking SQL out of the Oracle database underneath someone's desk and is putting it in the middle of the data lake house.
And it's super powerful when it's, you know, that kind of stuff.
So that's, that's what I was trying to nudge there.
And so less, yeah, less about SQL University.
Yeah.
Yeah.
You could probably tell the worst, mean, at this one, the basic SQL standpoint.
One of the reasons was, I didn't see what volumes were in a big SQL.
Unless that's because a lot of these topics have soldered into the broader, the existing killer that we have, which is you.
Dealing with stuff within Databricks, Databricks, PainPoint, etc.
So there's not much, in terms of search opportunity, I didn't see much.
I can always dig in more, but I didn't see much search volume over there, which is why I put SQL, because it's like, again, it's a very generic thing.

And it's really interesting content, but the companies that are appealing to data analysts and business analysts, they tend to have some version of the SQL University on there, even if they're not calling it that, obviously.
They do sort of address this content quite a bit.
So I just wanted to bring that up and make sure that we're not straying too far away from the data engineering side of things, but just wanted to note that this is an opportunity.
So, yeah, I just wanted to get your thoughts on that.
How we can strike a balance, maybe.
Can you, can we just, yeah, we definitely should take some cycles on this.
Can you help us understand some examples?
Because maybe there's a, maybe we can look at what targets are and see if we're in the right kind of neighborhood.
Sure, I can, I am putting together the assignment titles and everything.
I can just create a separate category for these.
Yeah, more, when you said people are creating universities, who is that?
What kind of click, you know, what are some of the search terms that are showing up?
Yep.
Maybe we can just do a quick, and if we need to maybe do that as a working session.
Because one of the things we don't want to do is, is make assumptions about our new audience.
And so if we have a chance to reach them, let's look at it.
Let's make sure we're taking a look at how that would work.
But again, my gut is...
There's a whole bunch of explainers about how to use SQLs, probably.
Yeah.
I don't think that's really, that's not really what we're about.
Yeah, of course.
It's too basic, but that's why I want to slag it, make sure, like, you know, get it.
Similarly, the BI tool comparisons, I think we can drop that.
Yeah, sure, this was, like, tangible versus whatever.
I think we can drop that since we're getting too far away.
I mean, we seem a bit unsure about it to begin with in terms of fit, et cetera.

The analysis types, which was this one down here, know, explainers on types of statistical analysis, I think we can hit a sort of a balance between this sort of academic explainer versus, you know, the broader, a broader scale, like explaining the impact of types of analysis on the organization.
I think we can hit that pretty well.
Generally, for this, the next step is I'm just going to review the final titles with the brief and any SEO information for a quick review, and then we can start getting these articles out.
The next thing was the ICP visits, which Ashleigh had sent over.
So I responded to that as well, but just want to flag this here.
So these blogs, these are the ones that were present in Evan's list from all published.
So some of these have had sort of quite a lot, like structured versus unstructured, for some reason, had like nine visits.
The ELT blog had like seven visits.
So, you know, this is basically the data we're seeing so far.
I have followed up with Evan regarding GA4 events, seeing if there's a better way to sort of design anything custom or track, like content downloads, et cetera, that I give us more insights.
But this is what we're seeing.
I guess the next step is just connecting this to data that you have internally and seeing the impact on your pipeline.
So that's where we're at with this.
That's interesting.
And thanks, Ashleigh, for pulling that out.
I think with this, it's actually more about, I mean, now we're getting into lots of different kinds of data, but we need some kind of summary.
In a summary table, this blog, because they're all different data points.
You know, this blog from search is found this way and had these clicks.
It's getting these visits.
It's here.
This data point isn't going to take us anywhere else.
This is just that data point.
There's no way to link this to anything else.
It's just a data point, though, that's important.
So, like, when you say, you know, structured versus unstructured data, nine, was that blog nine times more popular than the one?
Then the one?

Or it wasn't, and those topics spoke particularly, those topics did actually seem to reach people that we were trying to reach.
That's the next step we're going to work on.
I just wanted to flag this because we just got the data in today, so I was pretty curious to see what kind of visits we've been having.
That's terrific.
Generally, this is where, like you said, one data point.
We'll just add more columns and basically just see if we can build a funnel over here, see how the funnel's going.
Thanks.
So that's all we had for this week.

So...

Cool.
All right.
If there's no more questions, that's pretty much all we have for this meeting.
Awesome.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
Thank you.
Bye, everyone.
Thanks.
Thanks, guys.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Prophecy and GrowthX teams to discuss content strategy, performance, and upcoming tasks.](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content performance shows positive trends: clicks and impressions rising, with data engineering and governance topics performing well.](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
  - [Team aligning on content focus: balancing SQL education with Databricks SQL context, emphasizing pain points and Prophecy's solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=407.0)
  - [ICP visit data received: initial metrics shared, team discussing how to integrate this with other data points for more comprehensive insights.](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)

## Topics

### Team Updates and Introductions

  - [Mara Leighton introduced as new managing director at GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
      - [Background: 6+ years at Business Insider, 1.5 years at agency working with B2B startups](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=163.0)
  - [Vivek's parents visiting for a few months](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=27.0)

### Content Status and Performance

  - [3 articles in review, 5 published last week](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=195.0)
  - [1 article (Shadow IT) due for publishing this week, awaiting blog placement](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [Performance metrics:](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
      - [Clicks and impressions increasing](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
      - [Top performers: Data engineering skills, EPR blogs, Data Governance, data pipeline blogs](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)
      - [Medallion Architecture blog entering top 10](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [Upward trends in rankings across articles](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
  - [LLM referrals:](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
      - [Majority from Perplexity](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=350.0)
      - [Initial bump from Mistral, now stabilized](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)
      - [High engagement (NGATE sessions) indicating quality content](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=375.0)

### Content Strategy Discussion

  - [Aligning on SQL content approach:](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=407.0)
      - [Focus on Databricks SQL context and benefits for analysts](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
      - [Less emphasis on basic SQL education ("SQL University")](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=575.0)
      - [Team to review examples and search terms for potential topics](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=680.0)
  - [Dropping BI tool comparisons topic](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
  - [Balancing academic and practical aspects in analysis types content](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)

### ICP Visit Data

  - [Initial data shared for published blogs](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)
      - [Structured vs. Unstructured Data: 9 visits](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=811.0)
      - [ELT blog: 7 visits](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=820.0)
  - [Team discussing how to integrate this data point with other metrics](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=853.0)
  - [Next steps: Creating a summary table to compare blog performance across different data sets](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=927.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Review final titles, briefs, and SEO information for upcoming articles](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)
  - [Create separate category for SQL-related content examples](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=680.0)
  - [Schedule working session to discuss SQL content strategy if needed](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=690.0)
  - [Develop comprehensive summary table for blog performance metrics](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=927.0)
  - [Continue monitoring and analyzing ICP visit data](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)
  - [Deliver new article assignments for review by Monday next week](https://fathom.video/share/9Y1KczFTqDJzn1o4H3xfcesLqET3b_dV?tab=summary&timestamp=951.0)

action_items.description: Update checklist article with graphic design elements
Provide placement info for Shadow IT article on blog
Create separate category for SQL-related content examples; share with team
Review final titles, briefs, SEO info for new articles; send for team review
Follow up w/ Evan re GA4 events for content downloads & custom tracking
Create summary table combining search, clicks, visits data for blog performance analysis
